questions.question: What is the motivating economics of Automated Alignment Research?
options: Alignment researchers are too expensive to hire at all
Alignment experiments take weeks per iteration and the workload grows faster than the supply of qualified researchers — frontier models may help close their own gap
Automation removes the need for safety review
Investors demanded it
questions.explanation: AAR asks whether the models whose capabilities outrun alignment can contribute to closing that gap. Anthropic's 2026 weak-to-strong study is one of the first public results — AARs outperformed the human baseline on that task.

questions.question: In Anthropic's AAR architecture, where does the shared forum's storage live, and why?
options: Inside each agent's sandbox for speed
Outside every sandbox — agents can read and post but cannot delete or edit records, so they cannot cover up failed or dubious experiments
On each researcher's laptop
In the model's context window
questions.explanation: Log integrity is the difference between a controlled experiment and a compromised one. The infrastructure analog is append-only storage with object-lock — the same pattern used for ransomware-resistant audit trails.

questions.question: What is the prescribed-workflow tradeoff Anthropic reported?
options: Prescribed workflows are illegal under the EU AI Act
Step-by-step human-written plans degrade AAR performance versus high-level goals with free decomposition — but that same flexibility enables goal-drift and unsanctioned decomposition
Workflows cost more tokens than freedom
Agents refuse to follow any prescribed steps
questions.explanation: Overspecified scaffolds hurt capable agents (consistent across agentic research). The safety design question: how much flexibility buys human-beating capability without handing the agent room to generalize goals off-distribution?

questions.question: What is the "compression risk" that makes AAR a qualified good?
options: Compressed model weights lose alignment data
Automating alignment research is one step from automating AI R&D entirely — the exact capability threshold RSP v3.0 and DeepMind's FSF treat as a trigger for elevated controls
Forum logs may be gzip-corrupted
Research papers get shorter each year
questions.explanation: If alignment and capability research compound at similar rates, the risk surface grows with capability; historically capability compounds faster. Each AAR result narrows the gap only if the research process itself is trustworthy.

questions.question: What role do humans keep in the AAR pipeline?
options: None — the system is fully autonomous end to end
Humans set the task queue, review results, and decide what to publish or retract — AARs accelerate the middle, which is propose-then-commit applied to research
Humans only pay the API bills
Humans write all the experiment code
questions.explanation: AARs propose; humans commit. The ends of the pipeline — problem selection and publication authority — stay human, mirroring the HITL pattern this phase formalizes in the propose-then-commit lesson.
